briefing.headline: Rig up a Suspending Jerkbait — steady spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Suspending Jerkbait is a strong choice for spawn with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: Even in slick calm clear conditions, a jerkbait over the top of them will draw fish from distance. 'They're going to see it and they're going to come.' Don't switch to finesse just because it's calm.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Even in slick calm clear conditions, a jerkbait over the top of them will draw fish from distance. 'They're going to see it and they're going to come.' Don't switch to finesse just because it's c
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'Overcast + wind puts bass into feeding overdrive on clear reservoirs. Target main lake windblown points and where river channel pushes close to shore. Get deep jerkbait to maximum diving depth t
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: Guntersville's grass has evolved over the years: milfoil first, then hydrilla, now a lot of eel grass. All three still present. The grass creates great habitat and clear water (filters it). Floating eel grass cut up by wind/waves/boats makes ...

The D Shad's extra density allows use on casting gear (15-lb fluorocarbon, medium-heavy rod) unlike most soft jerkbaits — this opens up fishing thick cover without breakoffs, and enables skip casting under docks and overhangs

Palaniuk: 'A baitfish stuck in the gullet of a bass provides valuable information. Bait color and size are key factors when choosing jerkbaits. Mat shad works well for largemouth; in clear northern waters, half-tone translucent colors like pro blu...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable spawn conditions have fish committed to beds in 1-5ft. Focus on hard-bottom areas in protected coves, pockets, and flats. Polarized glasses are essential for sight-fishing.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish follow but won't commit, extend your pause to 8-10 seconds and vary the cadence
